action.reasoning: Observation: The model architecture has a persistent channel mismatch error in the decoder. The error occurs because I'm trying to concatenate features from layer3 (1024 channels) with upsampled features from layer4 (2048 channels), resulting in 3072 channels, but the decoder4 block expects only 2048 input channels. Looking at the forward method in the model, I can see the issue: `d4 = torch.cat([d4, x3], dim=1)` concatenates tensors with shapes [batch, 2048, H/16, W/16] and [batch, 1024, H/16, W/16], resulting in [batch, 3072, H/16, W/16], but decoder4 was defined with 2048 input channels. Analysis: I need to fix the channel dimensions in the decoder definition to match what the forward pass actually produces. Decision: I'll update the model architecture to fix the channel dimensions in the decoder blocks.
